title: Ease with Money in Motion
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring the difference between how receiving and spending feel, and what a fully easy relationship with both would look like
excerpt: A journal prompt set from the Day 79 theme of a 12-week money programme. The prompts open with a wide look at the feeling of money moving in both directions, then narrow to the specific story about holding on or bracing that is ready to be released.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 79 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to examine the difference between how it feels to receive money versus spend it, and what is keeping them uneven.

content.text: A journal prompt set from the Day 79 theme of a 12-week money programme. Day 79: lock in ease around receiving and spending. The prompts look at the specific quality of tension that appears in each direction of money flow, and what the story underneath it might be.
New to journal prompts as practice? Read
What thoughts or feelings come up when I receive money right now?
Excitement, guilt, disbelief, relief, none of these. Write whatever is actually there.
Notice whether the quality of feeling differs between the two directions.
Which is harder for me right now: receiving or spending? Why?
Write the actual reason, not the one that sounds good.
What story about needing to be careful, or holding on, am I ready to release?
Name the specific belief. Write it out plainly.
What recent transaction felt joyful or easy, proof that flow is already happening?
Find one specific example, however small.
Adapted from the Day 79 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
